California

Practices Cornmission
lilay 8, 1990
Danny Wa1sh
33 Payne River circle
sacramento, CA 95831
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-281
Dear ltr . Walsh:
This letter is in response to your request for confirmation
of oral advice provided to you lrhen you visited this office on
April 10, 1990, concerning your duties and respons ibi I i9ies under
the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the 'Act"1.r 
we do
not provide advice concerning provisions of 1aw other than the
Act. You may wish to consult rrith the Attorney General's Office
regarding other provisions of law such as Public contract code
Section 10411.
OUESTION
Do the provisions of the Act prevent you fron contracting
with the Ai,r Resources Board to act as the facilitator 
for the
Santa tlonica Bay restoration project?
CONCLUSION
The provj.sions of the Act do not prevent you from contracting
with the Air Resources Board to act as the facilitator 
for the
santa lilonica Bay restoration project.
rACTS
You are a forner menber of the water Resources control Board.
Your term expired in rebruary of 1990. As a nenber of that board,
you served as chairnan of the Santa l{onica Bay restoration
project. 
You are now a private consultant. The Air Resources
Board l{ould like to contract with you to act as the facil-itator
for the Santa llonica Bay restoration project. 
The Santa Monica
1 co\r.rn."nt Code Sections 81oOo-91015. Al.I statutory references
are to the Government Code unl,ess otherwise indicated.

regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et ses. A11 references to regulations are to Title 2,

File No. A-9 O-2 81
?age,
Bay restoration project is a joint federal and state effort
designed to assist with the cleanup of Santa Monica Bay. The
funds to pay for your services irouLd come from the Air Resources
Board,s budget. The Water Resources Control Board and the Air
Resources Board are separate state agenci-es.
ANALYSI S
The Act places certain restrictions upon trstate adninistra-
tive or-f ;-c:.al-srr who Ieave therr positj-on vj-th the state. 
rrstate
adniniscrative official'r neans every menber, officer, employee or
consultant of a state adninistrative agency who as part of his or
her official 
responsibi lities 
engages in any judicial, quasi-
judicial or other proceeding in other than a purely clerical,
secretarial or ministerial capacity. (Section 87400(b).) As a
nernber of the Water Resources Control Board and chairnan of the
Santa llonica Bay restoration project, you lrere a state administra-
tive official subject to the provisions of the Act. (Id.)
Sections 87401 and 87402 provide:
Section 874 01
No former state adninistrative officiat, 
after
the ternination of his or her employnent or tern of
office, sha1l for conpensation act as agent or at-
torney for, or othernise represent, any other
person (other than the State of California) before
any court or state administrative agency or any
officer or employee thereof by naking any fornal or
infomal appearance, or by naking any oral or srit-
ten conmunication with the intent to influence, in
connection with any judicial, quasi-judicial or
other proceeding if both of the follolring apply:
(a) The State of Cal.ifornia is a party or has
a direct and substantial interest.
(b) The proceeding is one in which the former
state administrative official participated.
Section 87402
No forner state administrative official, 
after
the temination of his or her enplolment or term of
office shall for conpensation aid, advise, counsel,
consult or assist in representing any other person
(except the State of california) in any proceeding
in which the official 
would be prohibited from ap-
pearing under section 87401.


File \o. A-9 0-2 81
?age l
Sections 87401 and 87402 prohibit you from representing, aid-
ing, advising, counseling, consulting, or assisting in represent-
ing any person,z for conpensation, in connection with any proceed-
ing in which you participated as a menber of the Water REsources
Control Board. Horever, Sections 87401 and 82402 do not prevent
you from contracting with the Air Resources Board or any other
state agency, whether or not the contract would j,nvolve natters on
which you worked as a state employee. Therefore, under the
provisions of the Act, you nay seek emplolment as a consultant and
represenc the interests of the Air Resources Board in matters
rei,ated to the Santa tlonica Bay restoratj,on project even though
you were previously involved with the saue project in your capac-
ity as a nember of the Water Resources Control Board.
As stated previousJ.y, this advice is linited to application
of the Act. we suggest that you contact the Attorney ceneralrs
office regarding other provisions of law which nay apply, such as
Public Contract Code Section 10411.
We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
should you have any further questions regarding this natter, do
not hesitate to call ne at (916) 322-59OL.
Sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

a2O47. ) Accordingly, Sections 87401 and 87402 restrict your abil-
ity to represent either individuals or other entities in certain
proceedings .
